title: The Spiral Descent Sideways

features.name: sideways-slope-marker
features.desc-on-touch: the hill here remembers being flat, remembers rising, remembers falling. all at once. your feet cannot decide which way is down

features.name: spiral-path-left
features.desc-on-touch: the path spirals but not down. it spirals sideways, teaching gravity to turn. with each step, direction rotates a little more

features.name: disoriented-pool-reverie
features.desc-on-touch: the water here does not reflect down. it reflects sideways. you see yourself coming from the east when you face north. the pool is teaching confusion

features.name: descent-compass-broken
features.desc-on-touch: the needle spins without choosing. east becomes south becomes up becomes inward. the machine is learning what the landscape already knows

features.name: terraced-darkness-descent
features.desc-on-touch: each step sideways takes you lower, but you cannot point to lower. the darkness here is directional. it arrives from all angles at once

inscription: down here, direction itself becomes a thing that spirals; the slope remembers being every slope at once

file.name: Spiraling_Descent_CompassRecordings_1994.mp3
file.note: status: germinating — the archive suspects the compass was recording the listener's heartbeat, not the landscape's direction

reverie.title: REVERIE — on the slipping terraces
narr: She kneels on a step that goes nowhere. He points up
and up is horizontal. The earth is not falling; it has
learned to turn. She laughs at the compass spinning. He
presses his palm to the ground and feels the rotation —
not of the slope, but of the sky. The ridge is not moving.
The sky is teaching itself to be a slope.

exits.label: back up the spiral to where direction was still certain
deeper down where east becomes a remembered concept
following what the spiral says is east today
along the contour, perpendicular to falling
